brands.name: Team Up Athletics
brands.notes: Utah-based mobile/home-based franchise selling custom team sports uniforms, apparel, and equipment to schools, leagues, and businesses. Founded 2017, franchising since 2022 — a small, fast-growing emerging system with a low capital bar and no national ad fund.

riskFlags.title: Very new, small franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2022 with roughly 39 units as of Entrepreneur's 2025 directory data; limited operating history as a franchisor and no published Item 19 earnings data found.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting unit counts across directories
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists 39 units (2025) while the IFPG broker listing shows 12 units (11 franchised, 1 company-owned), suggesting directory data lags or counts differ; verify current Item 20 in the FDD before relying on system size.

franchiseFee.note: Standard fee $35,000; disclosed range $35,000–$65,000 depending on territory/package

totalInvestmentMin.note: Low end = home-based model; older FDD summaries show up to ~$239K for a commercial showroom buildout

totalInvestmentMax.note: Per Entrepreneur's current directory data; 2024 FDD summaries showed $100K–$239K for commercial-location format

adFundPercent.note: No national ad fund / ad royalty disclosed

liquidCapitalMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $45K liquid / $55K net worth; other directories cite $40K–$70K

totalUnits.note: Per Entrepreneur 2025 directory; IFPG listing shows 12 (11 franchised + 1 company-owned)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2023 FDD on file)

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur shows strong unit growth (+550% trend) from a very small base

value: Initial HQ training with corporate sales ride-alongs
In-territory ride-along with seasoned sales professional
Ongoing streamed meetings, training modules, and direct corporate support

brands.name: Tempo by Hilton
brands.notes: Hilton's lifestyle-hotel franchise brand; franchisor is Hilton Franchise Holding LLC (franchising Tempo in the US since December 17, 2019). Hotel-scale capital requirement — total investment for a prototype 143-room hotel is in the tens of millions, excluding land. Only 4 franchised US hotels were open at end of 2024 but 48 agreements were signed and 70+ properties are in the global pipeline.

riskFlags.title: Hotel-scale capital bar
riskFlags.detail: 2025 FDD Item 7 estimates $23.18M–$57.81M total investment for a typical 143-room hotel, excluding real property. This is institutional/developer-scale capital, not a typical franchisee investment.
riskFlags.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo Franchise Disclosure Document, Item 7

riskFlags.title: Very young system — few open hotels
riskFlags.detail: Per 2025 FDD Item 20, only 4 franchised US hotels were open at end of 2024 (KY, NY, NC, TN); 48 franchise agreements were signed but unopened. Operating track record under the brand is thin, though backed by Hilton's systems.

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 and franchisee confidentiality provisions
riskFlags.detail: Hilton makes no financial performance representations for Tempo (no Item 19 data), and the FDD discloses that some current/former franchisees signed provisions restricting their ability to speak about their experience.

nationwide.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD (franchisor for the entire US incl. DC and territories; Exhibit L state effective dates; signed agreements across 21 states + DC as of Dec 31, 2024)
nationwide.note: Hilton franchises Tempo nationwide to qualified hotel developers/owners; development is deal-by-deal rather than territory-based

franchiseFee.note: Franchise Application Fee for new development/conversion: $100,000 plus $400 per guest room over 250. Change-of-ownership applications are $150,000 (not the standard new-development figure)

totalInvestmentMin.note: Estimate for a typical 143-room Tempo hotel, EXCLUDING real property/land

totalInvestmentMax.note: Estimate for a typical 143-room Tempo hotel, EXCLUDING real property/land; includes up to $419,447 payable to Hilton or affiliates

royaltyPercent.note: Monthly Royalty Fee: 5% of Gross Rooms Revenue

adFundPercent.note: Monthly Program Fee: 4% of Gross Rooms Revenue (funds marketing, reservations, and system programs; rate can be changed by Hilton)

totalUnits.note: 4 franchised US hotels open at Dec 31, 2024 (0 company-owned); 48 signed-but-unopened agreements. Hilton reported a global portfolio of 56 hotels trading + pipeline as of March 2025 and 70+ properties under development

closureCount.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD, Item 20 (2022–2024: no terminations, non-renewals, reacquisitions, or closures)

yearFounded.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD, Item 1 (brand trademarks filed Dec 16, 2019; brand publicly launched Jan 2020)

yearFranchising.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD, Item 1 ('We became the franchisor of Tempo Brand hotels for the US on December 17, 2019')

item19Available.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD, Item 19 ('We do not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance')

isGrowing.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD Item 20 (48 signed agreements) and Hilton newsroom (70+ properties under development; 2025 openings in Raleigh, Pigeon Forge, Manassas, Savannah)

multiUnitAvailable.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD, Item 6 (royalty modifications negotiated for multi-unit/experienced franchisees in 8 instances)

ownerTypes.source: Hilton 2025 US Tempo FDD (hotel franchise structure, Item 7 scale)

brands.name: Temporary Wall Systems
brands.notes: B2B rentable containment-wall (temporary wall rental) franchise for commercial construction and facilities; founded 2017, franchising since 2022, and owned by platform franchisor HomeFront Brands. Home-based, low-staff model with multi-territory packs; system grew quickly but parent-company turmoil surfaced in late 2025.

riskFlags.title: Parent company HomeFront Brands turmoil
riskFlags.detail: A December 2025 report documents rising franchisee dissatisfaction across HomeFront Brands systems including Temporary Wall Systems: ~4 TWS territories listed for resale, complaints of evasive corporate communication, pressure to sign updated agreements, and notable non-attendance at the November 2025 convention (mandatory $2,000 fee).

riskFlags.title: Item 19 historically based on a single affiliate operation
riskFlags.detail: The 2023 FDD's financial performance representation was based on one affiliate-owned location (operating since April 2017) spanning 5 territories — not independent franchisee results. Newer franchisee-level data should be checked in the current FDD.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk (2023 FDD review)

franchiseFee.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk (2023 FDD); FranchisePayback
franchiseFee.note: Single-territory fee commonly cited at $59,900–$69,900; IFPG's current listing shows $54,900 single and multi-packs from $99,900 (2-pack) to $189,900 (5-pack)

totalInvestmentMax.note: See min note for cross-source range

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk (2023 FDD); IFPG listing
royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 8% of gross revenue or a minimum royalty

adFundPercent.note: Brand fund 3% currently per IFPG/FranchisePayback; 2023 FDD set it at 1% with a 3% cap. Separate local advertising minimum (~$3,000/month) also applies

liquidCapitalMin.note: IFPG lists $150K liquid / $500K net worth; Vetted Biz cites $200K liquid / $400K net worth

totalUnits.note: 110 franchised, 0 company-owned (2024). IFPG separately reports 78 owners operating 310 territories (territory counting differs from outlet counting)

yearFranchising.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory; IFPG listing (first franchised units appear in 2022 per FDD Item 20)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk — 2023 FDD Item 19
avgUnitRevenue.note: Represents 2022 gross sales of $2,635,092 from a SINGLE affiliate-owned operation (open since April 2017) divided across its 5 territories (~$527K per territory). Not independent franchisee results

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk (2023 FDD Item 19); IFPG listing

fddAvailable.source: Free FDD Library / FDD review sites carry the FDD

exclusiveTerritories.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk (Designated Territory of ~4,000 target buildings)

trainingWeeks.source: IFPG listing (initial training program of approximately 14 days at corporate HQ)

isGrowing.source: FranchisePayback (8 franchised units in 2022 to 110 in 2024); company PR on nationwide expansion
isGrowing.note: Rapid growth through 2024; see riskFlags for late-2025 parent-company turbulence and territory resales

brands.name: Top Rail Fence
brands.notes: Residential/commercial fence installation franchise founded 2014, franchising since 2022 as part of platform franchisor HomeFront Brands. Grew fast (167 franchised territories by end of 2024) with a strong published Item 19, but December 2025 reporting documents significant franchisee dissatisfaction across the HomeFront system.

riskFlags.title: HomeFront Brands system turmoil and franchisee complaints
riskFlags.detail: December 2025 report: some Top Rail Fence franchisees grossing $1M+ say they are only breaking even (rising marketing costs, labor, mandatory warehouse overhead); 5+ territories listed for resale; owners report pressure to sign updated agreements that independent attorneys flagged as disadvantageous; notable non-attendance at the November 2025 convention. Parent also launched-and-shuttered several concepts (Yard Pros/Mozzie Dome, an electrician brand).

riskFlags.title: Wide performance dispersion in Item 19
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 (20 franchisees open 12+ months): top-third average gross revenue $2.23M vs bottom-third $497K — a 4.5x spread; gross margins ranged from 42.8% (top third) to 20.7% (bottom third). Median results may not reflect typical outcomes for new owners.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter — Top Rail Fence 2025 FDD review

franchiseFee.note: Standard single-territory fee; disclosed range $59,900–$73,900 (IFPG shows multi-territory packages up to $189,900)

totalInvestmentMax.note: See min note for cross-source range

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of a blended 5–7% of gross revenue or a minimum royalty of $300–$2,000/month depending on years in operation; 5% is the lower bound of the standard blended rate

adFundPercent.note: Brand Fund 2% of gross revenue; ALSO required: local advertising of the greater of 3% of gross revenue or $36,000/year, plus a $500/month marketing management fee

totalUnits.note: 167 franchised units at end of 2024 (0 company-owned); grew 21 → 93 → 167 over 2022–2024. IFPG currently reports 197 operating territories across 71 owners

yearFranchising.source: IFPG listing; Top Rail Fence joined HomeFront Brands in 2022 with launch of the franchise program (first 21 units in 2022 per FDD Item 20)

avgUnitRevenue.note: MEDIAN 2024 gross revenue of the 20 franchisees open 12+ full months. Thirds averages: top $2,228,190 / middle $921,141 / bottom $496,677. Gross revenue, not profit

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter — 2025 FDD review; IFPG listing

fddAvailable.source: FDD review sites (Franchise Chatter, Sharpsheets) summarize the 2025 FDD

multiUnitAvailable.source: IFPG listing (multi-territory fee tiers up to $189,900)

canRunFromHome.source: IFPG listing (requires physical location/warehouse); UnhappyFranchisee notes mandatory warehouse overhead

trainingWeeks.source: IFPG listing (5-day initial training: LMS, hands-on, technical, sales, leadership)

isGrowing.source: Franchise Chatter — 2025 FDD review (21 to 167 franchised units 2022–2024)
isGrowing.note: Strong unit growth through 2024; see riskFlags for late-2025 franchisee turmoil and resale listings

value: Initial training program covering operations, sales, marketing, and systems
Centralized call center for appointment setting
ongoingSupport.source: Top Rail Fence official franchising page

brands.name: Tox, The
brands.notes: The Tox is a lymphatic-drainage body-sculpting studio franchise (founded 2019, franchising since 2021) with company-owned studios in NYC, LA, and Boston plus a growing franchise base; franchise development runs through The Tox Franchising Group. Strong Item 19 AUV but it is derived from company/affiliate-owned studios, and 2024 saw two company-owned closures.

riskFlags.title: Company-owned studio closures in 2024
riskFlags.detail: Per FDD Item 20 data, company-owned outlets fell from 10 to 8 during 2024 (2 closures) even as franchised units grew from 1 to 6 — early signal worth monitoring in a very young system.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 franchise review (2025 FDD data)

riskFlags.title: Large gap between units awarded and units open
riskFlags.detail: IFPG reports 93 units awarded all-time but only 33 currently open (10 company-owned) — a substantial sold-but-not-open backlog typical of aggressive multi-unit sales in emerging brands; opening pace and franchisee capitalization should be verified in the current FDD.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 based on company/affiliate-owned studios
riskFlags.detail: The ~$1.12M AUV in Item 19 comes from 5 affiliate/company-owned locations operating without royalty obligations and without territorial restrictions; franchisee economics (8% royalty, 2% brand fund, heavy required digital-marketing spend) will differ.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 franchise review

franchiseFee.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review; IFPG listing
franchiseFee.note: Single unit $49,500; multi-unit: 2 units $91,500, 3 units $129,500, 4 units $165,500

totalInvestmentMin.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (2025 FDD Item 7)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (2025 FDD Item 7)
totalInvestmentMax.note: IFPG lists $448,000 as the top end

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (2025 FDD)
royaltyPercent.note: 8% of gross revenue, subject to a $1,000/month minimum

adFundPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (2025 FDD)
adFundPercent.note: Brand Fund 2% of gross revenue; ALSO required: minimum $1,500/month per digital channel for digital marketing and $2,750/month social media management per outlet ($3,500 setup)

totalUnits.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (FDD Item 20: 6 franchised + 8 company-owned at end of 2024)
totalUnits.note: FDD-verified count at Dec 31, 2024. IFPG's 2026 listing reports 33 units currently open (10 company-owned) and 93 awarded all-time

closureCount.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (company-owned outlets 10 to 8 during 2024)

yearFranchising.source: IFPG listing (first franchised outlet opened during 2022 per FDD Item 20)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (2025 FDD Item 19)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average total revenue (AUV) of 5 company/affiliate-owned studios that operate WITHOUT royalty obligations; average adjusted net income $326,128 (29.1%); avg service ticket $251.80; ~4.2 treatment rooms per studio. Not franchisee results

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter — The Tox 2026 review (Item 19 with AUV and net income)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2024 FDD on file); Franchise Chatter reviews 2025 FDD

veteranDiscount.note: $5,000 off the first unit's franchise fee

trainingWeeks.source: IFPG listing (2 days of 4-hour virtual ownership training plus 6-9 days in-person technique training)

isGrowing.source: Franchise Chatter (franchised units 1 to 6 during 2024); IFPG (93 units awarded)
isGrowing.note: Franchise side expanding rapidly; company-owned side contracted slightly in 2024 (see riskFlags)
